programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: In preparation for the spring cattle drive, Atz Lee, Otto and August survey the homestead for signs of bears, and use modern surveillance to track their movement

programme.title: Fish or Die
programme.desc: The intrepid anglers are in Borneo searching for the mahseer - a fish close to extinction. To find it, they risk entering the territory of the headhunting Dayak people

programme.desc: Marine biologist Greg Skomal and the Remus SharkCam team return to Guadalupe to find the worlds largest great white shark, Deep Blue

programme.desc: With a team member down, Russell and Mireya must forge ahead to hunt down leads, and their investigations uncover their first pieces of potential evidence

programme.desc: Top athletes including Olympic champion, Lindsey Vonn, team up with scientists to learn about the oceans top predators, using the latest satellite tagging technology

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How is one fish blowing scientists away with its brainpower? How have dogs learnt to melt our hearts? Do chimps laugh at the same jokes we do?

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Why domestic cats have trouble getting down from trees and why kiwi birds lay monster-sized eggs compared to their size

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Day-to-day life in a pitbull rescue centre. Tia gets a devastating injury, so her kids keep Villalobos running. An elusive dog needs catching in the middle of the night

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: An Italian familys bond is affected by cat Maxs behaviour. Plus, a woman moves in with a friend not knowing that the resident feline has psychotic tendencies

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Two best friends and room-mates clash when one is terrorised by the others cat, Buddha. Plus, a couple take to sleeping in separate beds because of troublesome feline Dylan

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Game wardens relocate a large herd of pronghorn to restore its numbers. They also investigate the killing of a protected rattlesnake and wrangle with a loose bull

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff fights to save a bearded dragon that is having a seizure, and Dr Amy is put to the test when a routine cat neuter turns complicated

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: The investigators respond to a report of a dead puppy in a yard, but when they get there they find the animal is still alive and race to save it

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe reveals why breathing is so important to becoming still and being in the moment, illustrated with natural history footage

programme.title: The Bridges That Built London with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: The historian explores the citys Thames crossings, discovering there is much more to them than simply a means of getting from one place to another. Beginning in the marshes of the estuary, he moves upstream to Vauxhall, where archaeologists have recorded Londons oldest bridge, and also learns about the legend of London Bridge, before reaching journeys end at the Millennium Bridge near St Pauls Cathedral

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner
programme.desc: Author Patrick Wright explores the architecture of Dorset, including the Church of St Peter and St Paul in Blandford, Milton Abbey, and the Old Higher Lighthouse at Portland

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: Water has always been the driving force in East Anglia - its ebb and flow has shaped the fenland. The ancient wetlands once covered hundreds of square miles, but today, well over 90 per cent has been drained and converted into farmland. Every winter, thousands of whooper swans return to the flooded pastures of the Ouse washes from their Arctic breeding grounds. On the Welney Wetlands, spring sees mad march hares boxing over mates. It was once thought these bouts were male hares boxing for dominance, but it is often the females throwing the punches to fend off the attentions of over-eager suitors. Narrated by Toby Jones

programme.title: Golf: The Players Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the prestigious tournament at TPC Sawgrass in Ponte Vedra Beach, Florida, which saw Rory McIlroy triumph in 2025 to claim this title for the second time in his career. The Northern Irishman defeated JJ Spaun in a three-hole aggregate play-off after both players finished on an overall score of 12-under-par after four rounds of play

programme.title: Irelands Treasures Uncovered
programme.desc: Documentary about the artefacts that have helped to shape and create modern Ireland, both north and south. Alice Roberts and Gavin Hughes uncover the tales behind treasures such as the Tara Brooch, the Broighter Hoard, the Waterford Charter Roll and others

programme.title: Call My Bluff
programme.desc: Robert Robinson presides over another duel of words and wits as team captains Frank Muir and Patrick Campbell are joined by guests Donald Churchill, Pauline Yates, Joanna Lumley and Frank Kearton. First broadcast in 1976

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Joyce Grenfell, Bernard Levin and Robin Ray. With guest musician Henryk Szeryng. First broadcast in 1979

programme.title: Imagine
programme.sub-title: Tracey Emin: Where Do You Draw the Line?
programme.desc: Alan Yentob follows the artist over the course of 12 months to chart her creative process, talking about her life to date and how she sees the rest of her career. Tracey discusses her troubled early years in Margate and the series of breakthroughs in the 1990s that saw her become a leading light of the Young British Artists, featuring career-defining work like My Bed and her embroidered tent Everyone I Have Ever Slept With 1963-1995. Contributors include Nicholas Serota, Jay Jopling, Maria Balshaw and David Dawson

programme.title: Emin/Munch: Between the Clock and the Bed
programme.desc: Edvard Munchs work has long inspired Tracey Emin, from her early video piece Homage to Edvard Munch and All My Dead Children to her monumental nine-metre high bronze sculpture The Mother. This documentary charts the affinities between Munchs art and Emins, exploring the ways that this godfather of expressionism resonates with one of Britains pre-eminent artists

programme.title: Nature and Us: A History Through Art
programme.desc: James Fox uses art to explore how humans began to try to understand nature for the very first time. From the Song dynasty in China and the Islamic world, through to the Scientific Revolution and the advent of the industrial era, he shows the very different ways in which humans came to both appreciate and understand nature

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe reveals why breathing is so important to becoming still and being in the moment, illustrated with natural history footage

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Joyce Grenfell, Bernard Levin and Robin Ray. With guest musician Henryk Szeryng. First broadcast in 1979

programme.title: Irelands Treasures Uncovered
programme.desc: Documentary about the artefacts that have helped to shape and create modern Ireland, both north and south. Alice Roberts and Gavin Hughes uncover the tales behind treasures such as the Tara Brooch, the Broighter Hoard, the Waterford Charter Roll and others

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the history of Charlestown in Cornwall, revealing how the villagess namesake Charles Rashleigh transformed the local trade and commerce from fishing and distribution of pilchards, to minerals exported by a hand-built port with pioneering technology. Ben looks at how Charles commissioned renowned engineer John Smeaton to create man-made lakes and hand-dug ponds, and explores their impact on the villages prosperity

programme.title: Wainwright Walks: Coast to Coast
programme.desc: Julia Bradbury follows in guidebook writer Alfred Wainwrights footsteps and walks across northern England, linking three national parks. She begins at St Bees Head on the coast of Cumbria, and has to deal with appalling weather conditions as she tackles her first Lakeland valley

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Daisy tries to encourage Onslow to notice her by picking up a toyboy in the hope of making him jealous. Meanwhile, Hyacinth forces Elizabeth and Richard to help her organise a social occasion at the church hall. Patricia Routledge and Judy Cornwell star

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy is sent to speak to the father of the boy bullying his godson - and discovers bad behaviour runs in the family

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: The story of Henry II, who built one of the greatest empires in the medieval world, only to see it torn down by members of his own family. Infamous for inciting the murder of Archbishop of Canterbury Thomas Becket, Henry has rarely been recognised for his achievements, which included the laying of the foundations for todays legal system

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Filmed over more than 20 years, this deeply personal documentary charts a father-son relationship that takes an unexpected and tragic turn. Norwegian film-maker Gunnar Hall Jensen has spent decades documenting his son Jonathans journey from childhood ambition to adulthood. As Jonathan grows older, the emotional distance between them widens

programme.title: Birdsong
programme.desc: Following 35-year-old ornithologist Sean Ronayne from Cobh, as he sets out on a mission to record the call of every bird species in Ireland. The programme also explores why Irelands ecological health is considered the worst in Europe, despite its reputation as the Emerald Isle

programme.title: Imagine
programme.sub-title: Tracey Emin: Where Do You Draw the Line?
programme.desc: Alan Yentob follows the artist over the course of 12 months to chart her creative process, talking about her life to date and how she sees the rest of her career. Tracey discusses her troubled early years in Margate and the series of breakthroughs in the 1990s that saw her become a leading light of the Young British Artists, featuring career-defining work like My Bed and her embroidered tent Everyone I Have Ever Slept With 1963-1995. Contributors include Nicholas Serota, Jay Jopling, Maria Balshaw and David Dawson

This documentary charts the affinities between Munchs art and Emins, exploring the ways that this godfather of expressionism resonates with one of Britains pre-eminent artists

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: From Strictly to the sofa, Claudia kicks off her chat show which promises Hollywood actors, the best of British and comedy stars - all put at their ease by the hosts trademark warmth, charm and humour. Her first guests are Hollywood star and musician Jeff Goldblum, actress Vanessa Williams, comedy icon Jennifer Saunders and funnyman Tom Allen

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Crimewatch Live
programme.desc: In 2005, two best friends died in an arson attack while sleeping, but it remains to be seen who lit the fire. Plus, a manhunt across continents to catch a dangerous criminal

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A first-time developer transforms a terrace property in Stoke-on-Trent, while more seasoned developers tackle projects in Leeds and Rugby in Warwickshire. Presented by Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Nick Hall help the teams in Staffordshire, and Christina Trevanion discovers the story of a First World War stretcher bearer during the Battle of the Somme

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: Ben wants to drop out of university to work full time on his online gaming channel, but his parents worry hes rushing into a decision. Bear and his team set the family a blindfolded navigation task to see how they communicate under pressure and a map-reading challenge. In a campfire chat, mediator Mo tries to help the family understand each others perspectives

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Spanish Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden revamps the second guest bedroom, while Alan Carr gets handed the short straw - the windowless bathroom. An old dividing wall that cuts a window in half is demolished and their efforts are rewarded when loads of natural light floods in. Away from the day job, Alan ropes in Amanda for a trip to one of Granadas famous hammam spas for some much-needed inspiration and RandR. The duo also head to Seville for a lesson in silk painting to create some statement bedside lampshades before arriving in one of Malagas top karaoke joints

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps globetrotter Sabiha find an Argyll and Bute retreat, with a line-up that includes a magical home on an island, just a short ferry ride from the mainland. He also meets a couple who made their own Argyll and Bute dream come true when they moved to a wonderful lochside croft

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea steps inside Glasgows Britannia Panopticon, the worlds oldest-surviving music hall, to help piano technician Jamie revive a 1904 Nilson pianola with roots in a New York speakeasy. Will Kirk is in Inverness to collect Debbies handcrafted chess-set, a family heirloom thats requires Kirsten Ramsays expert touch if its to be played with again

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong and Ed Gamble

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Its Mothers Day in Albert Square, and Lauren and Peter prepare to celebrate with the Beales at No 45. An upset Linda prepares to celebrate the day in the Vic with Elaine as Johnny is still distant with her, but things soon go from bad to worse when shes forced to invite Bea. Jean arrives back in Walford, but its clear there is still friction between her and Zoe over Jasmine

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: The singer and his nephew take a trip along the Atlantic coast, starting in Cork. They go foraging for edible plants and face off in the ancient sport of road bowling along the back roads of the port town of Castletown Berehaven. They also visit an enormous rewilding project area and on the Beara Peninsula and the countrys first Tibetan Buddhist Temple and Retreat Centre

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: BBC Medical Editor Fergus Walsh reports on the future of assisted dying in the UK, travelling to Western Australia, where voluntary assisted dying has been available since 2021 to meet one of the doctors who pioneered it, and to a patient preparing for an assisted death. He also talks to leading supporters and opponents in the UK, and visits a hospice to talk to patients at the end of their lives

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The semi-finals begin, with just eight contenders in the running. The first task requires them to deliver a no-waste dish, that makes use of every part of their chosen ingredients. With the four best dishes winning a place in the next round, the remaining chefs are challenged to produce a vegetarian meal

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Aontas (2025)
programme.desc: Drama about three unlikely thieves who make a disastrous attempt to hold up a rural Irish Credit Union. The story unfolds in reverse, going backwards through their recent lives revealing the circumstances that led them down this path and that caused their heist to go so disastrously wrong. Starring Carrie Crowley, Brid Brennan and Eva-Jane Gaffney

programme.title: Trying
programme.desc: During an activity break with the children, Nikki worries that she hasnt been able to bond with them as well as Jason has. Karen realises that she hates her job

programme.desc: Nikki and Jason prepare for Tylers seventh birthday party, but he is overwhelmed by all the attention. Karen finds a novel way to tell Scott he is going to be a father

programme.desc: How a far right extremist, who had printed his own gun, was jailed for terror offences. Plus, an appeal to help find the driver in a hit-and-run in Runcorn. Presented by Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts explores a two-bedroom semi-detached house with an impressive garden in Suffolk and a four-bedroom terrace property near Coventry, and Jacqui Joseph visits a mid-terrace cottage in Kent. Two homes are destined for the rental market, while the third becomes a labour of love for its new owner who plans to make it his home

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams scour an antiques fair for profitable bargains in Cheltenham, alongside experts Danny Sebastian and Caroline Hawley. Host Natasha Raskin Sharp oversees proceedings and also goes on a royal visit to see where Henry VIIIs sixth and final wife lived out the rest of her life

programme.desc: The adventurer tries to help couple Lisha and Jenna, whose different communication styles mean they are constantly fighting. They hope that time in the wild can help them repair their relationship

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Spanish Job
programme.desc: With a crumbling outbuilding and an overgrown vine out of control, Amanda Holden and Alan Carr raise the curtain on their biggest challenge yet as they take on the BandBs courtyard. Amanda has an idea to transform the space into a romantic movie set. Alan, however, is unconvinced by his friends vision. The duo meet up with tour guide and friend Bernado at the spectacular gardens of the Alhambra Palace to learn what plants thrive in the Spanish sun. Amanda gets blown away by local builder Rafas mosaic-filled cave house and ropes him in to help design a masterpiece of her own

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Williams heads for the Peak District, helping a Canadian couple spend up to £475,000 on a new home for them and their dog. Briony also meets some park rangers who keep the landscape in tip-top shape, including octogenarian Margaret who still volunteers on the hills

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea lends a hand with an 8,000-litre paint job and fixes a department stores pneumatic tube system. Will Kirk brings a shattered wedding bowl to colleague Kirsten Ramsay for a delicate restoration

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz, which sees contestants Steph, Joe, Paschal, Libby and Dalv race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: The decluttering team land in south London to help single mum Almarie and her 10-year-old daughter Marie overhaul their three-bedroom semi. With Staceys guidance, Almarie sorts through the belongings of her husband Marcus for the first time since his sudden death three years ago, creating space to honour old memories and create new ones. Back at the house Rob builds a stage for budding performer Marie and transforms an outhouse into an art studio

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: As the semi-finals continue, the chefs are put through their paces by chef John Chantarasak, who asks them to serve up a Thai-style dish inspired by the food that won him his Michelin star. After that, the judges ask the hopefuls to create a spectacular dessert, challenging them to reach a level that matches the worlds leading pastry chefs - with only the best going through the next round

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Comedy about five talented black teenagers at a prestigious boarding school. As the boarders gear up for their final term, a shocking act of vandalism throws everything into chaos. With the school on lockdown, Toby has to find a way to break out and see Abby. Leah is under suspicion for the school destruction, and Femi is distracted by a mystery girl. Jaheim wrestles with a dilemma that will change everything

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: The students are teamed up with career mentors, but things quickly go wrong. Leah is fuming with Felix about the sabotage, and Femis dreams are crushed. programme.desc: Zara McDermott discovers the dark side of Thailands backpacker culture. A visit to the Bangkok Immigration Detention Centre exposes how easy it is to get caught on the wrong side of the law

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: Zara McDermott meets men chasing cheap thrills in Asias sex capital, Pattaya, and also talks to a local sex worker, who reveals the harsh reality of working in the illegal sex industry. She then heads to Ko Phangan, an exotic island famed for its spirituality and beauty. For Zara, this is paradise - but Lucio, a Brit who built a burgeoning cannabis business here while the drug was decriminalised, reveals that navigating the local law isnt always easy

programme.title: Waterloo Road
programme.desc: Pregnant student Zoe causes problems for Jack, while Andrews new scheme to bring a little competition into the school backfires. Lorna considers having an abortion because of Toms apparent disinterest in being a father, and Chlo visits Donte in prison, where they discuss what will happen if Holly wakes from her coma and reveals the truth about the crash. Drama, starring Jason Merrells

programme.title: Waterloo Road
programme.desc: Izzies ex-husband Jimmy causes chaos at the school when he demands access to their children, and Tom confronts Lorna about her miscarriage. Meanwhile, the pupils set up a homework scam to gain more points in Andrews new house system, but it isnt long before their devious plot is uncovered. Jack wonders about the sudden improvement in the grades gained by Stephs French class

programme.title: The Jetty
programme.desc: Crime drama, starring Jenna Coleman as a detective who uncovers a dark web of secrets in the sleepy town shes always called home. Recently widowed DC Ember Manning suspects there is a predator targeting underage girls. She finds herself reflecting on the age difference between herself and her late husband

programme.title: The Jetty
programme.desc: Still reeling from her shocking discovery, Ember is desperate to find out what Riz knows about the Amy Knightly case. They begin to share information and Ember tells Riz her suspicions about the identity of Mirandas abuser. Ember is horrified when shes told that the lake is to be searched for Amys remains. When Riz stops returning Embers calls, Ember suspects that its because she has uncovered the terrible truth about what happened to Amy. Crime drama, starring Jenna Coleman and Weruche Opia

programme.title: Caitlin Hanna: Trafficked in Belfast
programme.desc: Documentary about the life and sudden death of a young Belfast woman and how it fuelled a three-year police investigation that exposed a hidden criminal underworld operating in Northern Ireland. Twenty-one-year-old Caitins life was tragically cut short, leaving her mother Deborah and her friends searching for answers. What they didnt know was that Caitlin has been exploited by a criminal enterprise, where she and many other young women were groomed, controlled and fed drugs by ruthless men

programme.title: Men of the Manosphere
programme.desc: James Blake enters the virtual world of manhood, often referred to as the manosphere, in search of status, wealth and relationships. He meets three young men who have turned to male social media influencers for guidance and instruction as they seek to better themselves and achieve success in different areas of their lives, both on and offline

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy realises just how far he and Molly have come since the separation, but Johns worries grow as Tommy takes on a 100km triathlon, pushing his body to new physical limits. Before the challenge, Tommy takes some downtime at the spa with his mates, preparing for a huge press push ahead of his series premiere. After completing a series of intense physical challenges, he has to deal with a social media backlash

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy receives news of the untimely death of boxing legend Ricky Hatton, which reminds him to live life to its fullest. He then sets out on the most gruelling test of endurance possible - climbing the three highest mountains in England, Scotland and Wales in just 24 hours. At Halloween, Tommy and John go pumpkin picking with Bambi, things are back on track with Molly, and the house renovation is almost completed

programme.desc: Gay rights activists gather in London for a Pride march in 1984, and decide they have a common cause with the miners strike. Fact-based drama, starring Ben Schnetzer, Bill Nighy, Andrew Scott and Imelda Staunton

programme.desc: In a dystopian near future, the Gateway offers the only chance of survival for teenagers Zero and Serger and their younger sister Wake, who they are determined to save from the violence of the City. When Zeros life is threatened, she flees with Wake, while Serger is forced to chase them down. Faye Gilberts thriller, starring Lauren Grace

programme.title: Small Prophets
programme.desc: Strange things are happening in Marvin Gardens and they are starting to attract unwanted attention. Gordon threatens to fire Michael, while Brian has received a letter from an old friend, and a hole in the roof leads to a discovery. Kacey and Michael exhume the prophets from their pile of manure to see if they have reached the state of divination and the ability to predict the future. Comedy written and directed by Mackenzie Crook, starring Pearce Quigley and Lauren Patel

programme.desc: Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Steven Bartlett are joined by beauty mogul Jenna Meek as they grill the latest business hopefuls. Pitching to the Dragons this time - a founder from Northern Ireland presents her snack range designed to unleash the natural power of oats. A father-and-son duo unveil their lockdown-born DIY gadget. A software developer from London brings a real-world hide-and-seek adventure with a tech twist. And a physiologist from Lancashire hopes to turn heads with her protective, sun-shielding lip care range that doubles as everyday cosmetic skincare

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Tottenham Hotspur at Anfield, and Manchester United v Aston Villa at Old Trafford. Liverpool won 2-1 against a Spurs side that ended the game with nine men in the reverse fixture, but the Londoners will have been determined to avoid another defeat to ease their relegation concerns. Villa beat United by the same score when they last met, with Morgan Rogers scoring a goal in either half to inspire them to victory. Plus, Crystal Palace v Leeds United, and Nottingham Forest v Fulham

programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the antiques team value finds in Selby, North Yorkshire, turning up Masonic porcelain, a picture embroidered from sock-wool in memory of the gallant Captain Oates and a personalised copy of Beatrix Potters book The Fairy Caravan

programme.desc: Leaving Kinlochleven, Paul Murton discovers a forgotten First World War German prison camp, before coming across a colossal memorial in concrete to the last of the navvies. After failing miserably at clay pigeon shooting at Corrour Lodge, Paul makes a long hike down to Loch Laggan, where he is taken out to an island with a legendary feasting tradition

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Amol Rajan asks the questions as two teams who lost their first match return to play a second quarter-final, with both needing to win this one to stay in the competition

programme.title: Inside the Rage Machine
programme.desc: The BBCs social media investigations correspondent Marianna Spring uncovers the inside story of how social media algorithms designed to connect people have been helping to tear them apart. Insider testimony and whistleblower documents expose a machine thriving on outrage and division as part of a business model, with radicalisation, real-world violence and fractured societies some of the consequences of a system built to shape how users think, feel and see the world

To prove his point, he uses a pseudonym to write his own outlandish book, which propels him into the heart of hypocrisy and the madness he claims to disdain. Satire, starring Jeffrey Wright

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith and Vick Hope explore sugar beet harvesting in the east of England, 100 years after sugar was first grown in this country. In Norfolk, Vick meets farmers racing to lift the last of this years crop, while Charlotte visits one of the countrys biggest processing plants. Plus, Tom Heap investigates whats really in the sewage sludge spread as fertiliser

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey and her team of experts are back to help more families declutter their homes. They begin with the Kings in Fife - retired architectural technician Gerry, care worker Trish and two of their three grown-up children. Their house contains 30 years of memories, knick-knacks and general clutter - not to mention countless unfinished DIY projects. During the process, the team learn Gerry has recently been diagnosed with early onset dementia, so it is important they hold on to items that will continue to spark memories for him while losing any clutter so it is easy for him to move around

The other items are a cherished garden bench given to the owner by her late husband, a camera used to capture images of the Second World War and a 1940s fishing chart that helped the original owner - a Grimsby trawlerman - navigate the North Sea for more than 50 years

programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: Chess Masters: The Endgame
programme.desc: Sue Perkins hosts this high-stakes contest following 12 rising stars of the UKs chess community as they battle it out in a series of puzzles and eliminators. The contest kicks off with the first of two heats, which sees six plays from across the UK arrive in Cardiff where they face a brain-boggling puzzle and a high-stakes game of chess

programme.title: Clive Myries Italian Road Trip
programme.desc: The newsreader travels around the country, immersing himself in Italian culture and sampling its renowned cuisine, beginning in the south-eastern region of Puglia. Clive discovers how the James Bond franchise helped turn an abandoned town into a world heritage site, finds out why Puglia is the gay holiday capital of Italy and learns to play in a traditional pizzica band

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: Dramatised natural history show featuring a cast of real animals interacting on the African savannah, narrated by Star Wars actor John Boyega. Lioness Kali has broken the golden rule of family life by having cubs with an outsider, and when she tries to bring the young ones home she is forced into exile. Passionate baboon Bakari starts a deep and jealous rivalry after the female he was devoted to is stolen away by the new leader of the troop, and teenage elephant Tembo feels sidelines when his mother gives birth to a new baby

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the team visit Wisbech in Cambridgeshire, where a Martinware bird, thought by its owner to be a tobacco jar, turns out to be anything but. A rare ring proves to be an interesting find and a selection of royal letters, invitations and sketches collected by a Windsor Castle librarian in the 1860s reveal a secret history

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Hundreds of people arrive with their antiques and collectibles at Barrow Hill Railway Heritage Centre in Derbyshire, where experts seek out some interesting items to take off to auction. Meanwhile, Paul Martin discovers more about the history of the venue

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Steve Backshall, Steve Pemberton, Sam Quek and Rachel Riley face off, demonstrating the general knowledge and trivia skills that made them champions last time

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and tour guide Paul Gorton test Susan Calman, Max Fosh and Noreen Khan on their knowledge of Enniskillen in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs from Wales serve up their canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry. The dishes are judged by Michelin-starred chef Tommy Banks, and include two celeriac dishes, one honouring Under Milk Wood and Richard Burton and the other TE Lawrence. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Archaeologists in Somerset are blown away by the discovery of an entirely new Roman town in the intended pathway of some new electricity cables. Multiple, impressive layers of road building from the Roman era are revealed, in particular a spectacular section from the fourth century when Emperor Constantine presided over a boom in Britains economy. Elsewhere in Pembrokeshire, Alice Roberts revisits Whitesands Bay where a team are excavating a mysterious collection of child burials

programme.title: An Bothy Band
programme.desc: Irelands foremost traditional music band burst into life in late 1974 and although their star radiated for barely five years they released four albums to wide critical acclaim, gaining enormous popular status. With special interviews and behind-the-scenes rehearsal footage, this documentary looks into the bands legacy as surviving members Matt Molloy, Donal Lunny, Tríona Ní Dhomhnaill, Kevin Burke, Paddy Glackin and Paddy Keenan reunite for a live performance. In Irish

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events with Faisal Islam

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Europe on the Edge with Katya Adler
programme.desc: Katya continues her journey through Spain, where she trains with a team of firefighters and learns how the country is spending billions to fight climate change - money that is coming out of the national defence budget. She then travels to France, witnessing a rocket launch in French Guiana, and learning more about the European Space Agency, which has its base in Paris. In the capital, she also finds out about the countrys generous welfare state, before venturing into the countryside to meet farmers who are fed up with European regulation

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: In the early hours a frantic teenage girl calls 999 to say that her friend has been attacked and stabbed in a car park. Police rush to the scene, where they find a 16-year-old with a life-threatening wound to his stomach. While his life hangs in the balance, West Midlands Polices homicide team get to work, with forensic investigators discovering two discarded weapons at the scene - a kitchen knife and a machete

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: Lord Sugar tasks the candidates with making a demo for their own virtual reality fitness brand that packs a punch and scores big on investments. Both teams work up a sweat but in the boardroom theres nowhere to run

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: As Alan Sugar continues his search for a new business partner, Angela Scanlon and her guests take a deep dive into the candidates performances and analyse the latest task, and the drama of the boardroom. Plus, a chat with the latest person to leave the process

programme.title: We Might Regret This
programme.desc: A benefits assessor comes to evaluate Freyas needs and determine how many hours of PA financial assistance she will receive. Jo unexpectedly turns up at the venue for Janes book launch to try to make some progress with a clearly wounded Levi

programme.title: AI Confidential with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: The mathematician explores the link between a controversial AI algorithm and Luigi Mangione, the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson. There were allegations the company used artificial intelligence to deny insurance claims from vulnerable patients, while Mangione himself studied AI at university. Hannah travels to the US to meet a woman who lost her husband and claims it happened after he was denied healthcare by an algorithm, and she also speaks to a UnitedHealthcare insider who reveals her concerns about their use of AI

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: UFO Encounters UK
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sixty years on, Tonys close encounter has become an obsession. Pilot David presents photographic evidence of the UFO he almost crashed into while flying near Area 51

programme.title: UFO Encounters UK
programme.desc: Part two of two. Adam, Gwen and Julie recall personal encounters with UFOs, before experts theories are put in doubt when it is revealed that each individual has time missing from their plotted recollections

programme.title: Monster Aliens
programme.desc: Investigating monster tales told throughout history and whether the explanation could be extraterrestrial

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Alien Pilots, Carolina Lights and Stone-Cold Animals
programme.desc: An examination of several seemingly impossible things caught on film. This episode features footage purportedly of alien pilots in a UFO cockpit. Plus, the claims that a Australian man stumbled upon an alien corpse on the pavement

programme.title: Irish Pickers
programme.desc: Ian and Butzy cross the Irish Sea to the north of England, where destinations include Manchester Uniteds Old Trafford, the home of one of Irelands sporting greats - George Best

programme.title: Irish Pickers
programme.desc: Ian Dowling and Butzy reach Northern Ireland, finding a treasure trove of maritime memorabilia in Belfast, bidding for some Croke Park turnstiles in Cavan and visiting the recreation of a traditional rural Irish home in Ulster

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson visits Caernarfon, north Wales, where Michael Hogben is accused of being stingy by an unhappy lady, and Karen Dalmeny deals with a feisty girl with a sword. programme.title: Junk and Disorderly

With hopes high after two tough weekends, they set their sights on a big sale at a mega jumble held by Sammy Miller - will they finally find success?

programme.desc: The team investigates a large, invisible bubble anomaly using drones, specifically within the Triangle area of the ranch. The analysis involves aerial experiments and the use of LiDAR scanning to map the phenomenon

programme.title: Historys Greatest Myths
programme.desc: The themes of society and myths that engulf memories of the past. These myths often become woven into the fabric of society and can pass through generations. Myths surrounding feminism and the suffragettes still swirl, while links between these myths can also be seen through the stories of the Salem Witch Trials. Suffering is a recurring theme throughout the ages and this is explored in the myths of the bloody murders of Jack the Ripper and suffering as a result of the Stock Market crash

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: As a challenge, Ray writes a moving eulogy for Frank - despite the obvious fact he is still alive. Ray Romano and Peter Boyle star

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray thinks something is wrong and confides in Debra when he has a miserable night with his friends, but an invitation to join Frank for a steam bath restores his spirits. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Peter Boyle

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra give Robert $1,000 to help him out of dire financial straits - but he takes advantage of their generosity by jetting off for a holiday in Las Vegas

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Samantha ends her relationship with Frasier on their return from a seemingly perfect weekend away, leaving him desperate to find out where he went wrong. However, Niles helps him realise that he only liked his new woman for her famous friends. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A story about the holiday season is told from the perspectives of Daphne, Niles, Martin and Roz as the Crane family prepares for Christmas. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer, John Mahoney and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: Where Every Bloke Knows Your Name
programme.desc: A British theme pub becomes Frasiers favourite new haunt - much to the annoyance of Daphne, who is already a regular and dislikes having to share the place with her boss. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Jane Leeves

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink has to choose whether to return to his heartless former agent Bebe, or stick with his current representative, well-meaning but ineffectual Ben. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef tries to help the owners of Iberville Cuisine and Oyster Bar, in the heart of the French Quarter in New Orleans. They want to be reinvigorated before Super Bowl LIX kicks off in the citys Caesars Superdome - and the tourist trade that comes with hosting the biggest sporting event in America

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Midday news summary

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien head to Tiverton in the West Country to meet petrolhead Roger. Henry finds part of a tornado jet that he wants to turn into a table, while Simon finds a pair of exhaust pipes, which he thinks could become a trendy pair of lamps

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programme.desc: Documentary following the Countryfile presenter as he and his family come to the rescue his parents farm in the Durham hills after his mother suffers an accident

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Nicki Chapman in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps Mandy and Mick, acouple who dream of owning a holiday home on the sunny southern Costa Blanca, that they can move to permanently once they are both retired

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Renovation rookies Angie and Ash dream big in the Italian Alps. In France, neighbours Aggi and Camille join forces to turn around a derelict restaurants fortunes. Sparks fly in Portugal, as Anglo-American couple Harriet and Mondo fight to boost business at their holiday cottages

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first venue of the week is Hotel Penzance in Cornwall, where owners Louise and Daga hope to wow their guests with breathtaking views over St Michaels Mount. An afternoon on the water brings with it revelations that make waves, and comments about TV troubles get a bad reception

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Teaching assistant Michelle hosts the first party from Bedfordshire and Cambridgeshire, hoping to share her Portuguese and American heritage as well as dishes from Italy

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: What Happened Next?
programme.desc: The presenters catch up with house-hunters who bought their perfect place abroad. Jean Johansson revisits Lisa and Rob in southwest Frances Charente region, while Jasmine Harman heads to the Andalucian countryside of Velez-Málaga to see how Alison and her daughter Paula are getting on

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Political Slot
programme.desc: Danny Kruger, MP for East Wiltshire, sets out Reform UKs plans to provide more support for Britains veterans

programme.title: Batch from Scratch: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Suzanne Mulholland and Joe Swash help a single mother in Aberdeen, who is struggling to get her kids into healthy eating habits, regularly prepares multiple meals, and often doesnt have time to sit down at the table to eat herself. Suzanne and Joe create a plan to cut costs, reduce sugar, simplify dinnertimes and get everyone involved, kids included

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: With the final getting ever closer, the handcuffed couples face a new challenge: their first holiday away together. Jonathan Ross sends them across the UK on iconic British breaks, from beautiful canal trips to camping in the wild countryside, and enjoying all the fun of the fair at the great British seaside. On neutral territory, and without friends and family around, how do they cope with just their cuff-partner for company?

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Sisters Caitlin and Lucy have never been in a romantic relationship with a guy. They decide to visit the restaurant to each take a dive into dating. Naked cleaner Elleigha is confident in everything - except romance. Shes matched with head chef Louis, a man searching for a wild connection with someone with strong opinions. Aspiring erotic-fiction-writer Immogen also attends, finally ready to live out her passions in real life

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Sarah from Plymouth becomes the first returning picker having previously been on the show as David. Now proudly pansexual and living as a woman, Sarah cant wait to road test her new body and hopes her first date as a woman will be everything she has dreamed of. Twenty-year-old plumber Jamie from Swansea is fed up with being hit on by OAPs at work, he comes into the studio looking for love

programme.title: The Eclipse
programme.desc: Mystery drama set in the Aubrac region of France, focusing on events taking place in the picturesque village of Bozouls. The community is buzzing with activity. Manue and Johanna, both gendarmes, get together for the big party to celebrate the eclipse. However tragedy strikes when Nour, the daughter of new rural settlers, disappears after a terrible accident. Manue and Johanna investigate the disappearance, which involves their children. With Anne Charrier, Claire Keim and Hubert Delattre. In French

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.sub-title: Lido, Anna Vincenzos and Le Bistro
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three of the restaurants he tried to save, returning to Lido, Anna Vincenzos and Le Bistro to find out whether his advice has been put to good use

programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: Brenda Blethyn and Jessica Reynolds star in this drama based on the bestseller by Barbara Taylor Bradford, charting a womans rise from maid to businesswoman. In early 1900s Yorkshire, ambitious maid Emma Harte dreams of escaping Fairley Hall for a better life, but is caught up in a forbidden romance with Edwin Fairley, son of the master of the house. In 1970s New York, the worlds richest woman is warned that a plot is brewing to take away the empire she has worked so hard to build. With Ewan Horrocks, Will Mellor, Leanne Best and Lydia Leonard

programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: As housemaid Emma and young master Edwin fall deeper in love, she struggles to see how they can overcome their class differences. Meanwhile, Adam and Olivia continue their own love affair as Adeles erratic behaviour and paranoia worsen. In the 1970s, 80-year-old Emma reckons with her childrens plan to oust her from the Harte empire - unaware of a secret romance blossoming between her protegee granddaughter Paula and Jim Fairley. Starring Jessica Reynolds and Brenda Blethyn

programme.title: Stacey Solomons Renovation Rescue
programme.desc: Stacey Solomon rescues homeowners whove been left high and dry by their builders, costing them their hard-earned cash and leaving them in chaos. Stacey shares her infectious enthusiasm for DIY and her clever money-saving design tips, helping families salvage their half-built home without breaking the bank. Stacey also upscales her own building skills, mastering everything from plumbing to micro-cementing, and puts all this building knowledge into practice at her own family home, Pickle Cottage. Erick and Carolines 1980s bungalow extension in Enfield has left them seriously out of pocket and downhearted. They had a vision of a Japandi - a Japanese/Scandinavian-inspired open-plan kitchen, dining and living area and a colourful bedroom. But its in jeopardy after they lost tens of thousands of pounds on building work that had to be re-done, leaving them facing a massive overspend. Staceys determined to help them realise their dream, by showing them how to tackle jobs themselves to help them save money. Stacey also makes a start on her own DIY project, building a wall in her garage to separate her home gym from her husband Joes fishing gear. Then she helps Erick and Caroline get to grips with plumbing, floor-fitting and laying decking. And she also brings them beautiful, achievable DIY design ideas including a stunning oak-panelled room divider and a striking colour-blocking paint scheme for their bedroom

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: A new cellmate spells danger for Lucas, forcing Dillon to take desperate measures. Theresa pushes Mercedes to give Warren another chance. Diane and Tony continue to lie to protect their loved ones. And a residents romantic hopes end in betrayal. Writers: Emily Gascoyne, Gary Sewell; Dirs: Duncan Foster, Eleri B. Jones; Prod Co: Lime PicturesShort: A new cellmate spells danger for Lucas, forcing Dillon to take drastic action. Theresa pushes Mercedes to give Warren another chance. Diane and Tony lie to protect their loved ones.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Cliff claims Adolf Hitler is living in his apartment building and Carlas lover John Allen Hill has a heart attack, making her question how she really feels about him. Bar-room comedy, starring Ted Danson and Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Rebeccas father visits the bar and tries to persuade her to return to San Diego to live with the family again. Comedy, starring Ted Danson, George Wendt, John Ratzenberg and Kirstie Alley

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray impulsively agrees to start sharing the childrens washing facilities so that Debra can have the bathroom to herself - but soon regrets acting so hastily. Ray Romano stars, with Patricia Heaton and Peter Boyle

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra and Ray happily befriend a new couple - whose annoying eight-year-old son Spencer insists on making a nuisance of himself. Ray Romano stars

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert becomes convinced he has found the perfect woman, but Ray witnesses something that makes him think otherwise. Brad Garrett and Ray Romano star

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink takes everyone to a ski lodge for a weekend break, but wires are crossed and everyone gets the wrong impression about one another. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.desc: Lilith (Bebe Neuwirth) arrives out of the blue and tries to rekindle her relationship with Frasier. He manages to resist her charms, but Niles is not as strong-willed. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A cousins wedding reunites the Cranes with a Greek branch of the family - and forces Frasier to deal with an old grudge against his aunt Zora. Kelsey Grammer stars, with a guest appearance by Broadway star Patti LuPone

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A new radio personality becomes the talk of the town, but Frasier takes an instant dislike to him. Martin is thrown out of Niles favourite shop - the only local establishment that sells Eddies preferred brand of dog food. Comedy, guest starring Bill Campbell

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay continues to help the lost owners of Iberville Cuisine, in the French Quarter of New Orleans. Will the staff have to walk out and implement their plan B?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien head to Worcestershire to meet mechanic Adrian. Simon restores an old, wrecked armchair into some punk-rock furniture

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programme.desc: Matt calls in old family friend Richard and his heavy machinery to take on his biggest plan to date - a new road around the farm, hopefully making everything more accessible for his parents. The list of repairs keeps growing and Matt finds himself in a race against the rain to fix a hole in the roof of the new chicken barn, but the family get a welcome break from work when the puppies are born and Molly, Luke and Nicola get to meet the new arrivals for the first time

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with Nicki Chapman joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Therapists Bernie and Vernon, who first met 15 years ago, are searching for their perfect holiday home on the Canary Island of Fuerteventura. They love travelling, and although Vernons never actually been to Fuerteventura before, theyre convinced its the perfect place for their holiday home. Sara Damergi shows them the best of what their £150,000 budget can buy; will any of her options hit the mark?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Seasonal deadlines crank up the heat for restaurateurs Aggi and Camille in France and hospitality rookies Angie and Ash in the Italian Alps. The summer strain also bites for Harriet and Mondo at their Portuguese holiday cottages

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Twins Sara and Paula invite their rivals to their boutique hotel in Devon, The Horn of Plenty, where Louise and Daga find plenty of things to be disappointed about in their room. An afternoon at a local vineyard seems to go down well - but are there sour grapes in the feedback?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the second evening in Bedfordshire and Cambridgeshire, self-styled culinary explorer Dave serves up a daring menu melding flavours that he claims no one else would ever think of combining. But it soon becomes clear why that is

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: What Happened Next?
programme.desc: Ben Hillman is back on Lanzarote to find out how Nicola and Paul are getting on with the holiday home he helped them to find in the resort town of Puerta del Carmen. Leah Charles-King takes a windswept walk on the Costa del Sol with Kim, who found a dream holiday home for £140,000

programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Political Slot
programme.desc: Ken Skates MS, Member of the Senedd for Clwyd South, sets out Welsh Labours plans to improve the transport network in Wales

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Documentary set inside Woodgreen animal charity, following staff who are committed to matching their homeless dogs with prospective owners. The Bodley family face double trouble as they meet cockapoo twins Milo and Mable, and Floyd the lurcher arrives at Woodgreen in need of some serious TLC. Willis, the three-legged Pomeranian, tries to befriend Dory the stray terrier

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: Jonathan Ross presents the final of the reality challenge. With £100,000 at stake, the remaining couples take part in several challenges designed to test their friendships to the limit. Along the way, some of them are eliminated, leaving only the strongest to take on the final test - a race from Scotland to London with no phones and no money

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on shows including The Assembly, Genius Game, The Piano, Scam Interceptors, The Americas and Married at First Sight Australia. They also consider a BBC News report on noisy phones

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on The Capture, Married at First Sight Australia, Louis Theroux: Inside the Manosphere, The Floor, Will and Ralf Should Know Better, Britains Best Mattress: How to Sleep Better and This Morning (AI boyfriend)

programme.title: Open House: The Great Sex Experiment
programme.desc: Childhood sweethearts Natalie and Tom from Yorkshire arrive at the retreat wanting to spice up their 12-year relationship. Their mismatched sexual histories mean that Natalie arrives with a confidence that Tom might be unable to match. After a flirtatious evening leads to steamy intimacy in the hot tub with residents Josh and Holly, the couple are left questioning everything. Meanwhile, Chanice and Richardo invite 15 of the residents upstairs for an orgy, though being in a room with so many people having sex proves overwhelming for Richardo, so a once-in-a-lifetime experience seems set to pass the couple by

programme.title: Confessions of a Cam Girl
programme.desc: Part one of two. The stories of five women selling explicit videos online. The market has exploded since lockdown, leaving them struggling to find a niche among their competitors. Maya uses her earnings to support her family financially, but is feeling burned out by the pressure to produce as much content as possible, while Sarah has been invited to attend her first-ever photoshoot in Marbella

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Pasadena, California, where he visits Jamaican-themed restaurant Cafe Kingston. The business in run by Dr Una Morris, a micro-managing owner who also works at a radiotherapy clinic next door, and is determined to introduce hospital-style techniques to the kitchen by sealing everything in plastic bags

programme.title: The Last Leg

programme.desc: The fiery chef tries to help the owners of Iberville Cuisine and Oyster Bar, in the heart of the French Quarter in New Orleans. programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Renovation rookies Angie and Ash dream big in the Italian Alps. In France, neighbours Aggi and Camille join forces to turn around a derelict restaurants fortunes. Sparks fly in Portugal, as Anglo-American couple Harriet and Mondo fight to boost business at their holiday cottages

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first venue of the week is Hotel Penzance in Cornwall, where owners Louise and Daga hope to wow their guests with breathtaking views over St Michaels Mount. An afternoon on the water brings with it revelations that make waves, and comments about TV troubles get a bad reception

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Teaching assistant Michelle hosts the first party from Bedfordshire and Cambridgeshire, hoping to share her Portuguese and American heritage as well as dishes from Italy

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: What Happened Next?
programme.desc: The presenters catch up with house-hunters who bought their perfect place abroad. programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Political Slot
programme.desc: Danny Kruger, MP for East Wiltshire, sets out Reform UKs plans to provide more support for Britains veterans

programme.title: Batch from Scratch: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Suzanne Mulholland and Joe Swash help a single mother in Aberdeen, who is struggling to get her kids into healthy eating habits, regularly prepares multiple meals, and often doesnt have time to sit down at the table to eat herself. Suzanne and Joe create a plan to cut costs, reduce sugar, simplify dinnertimes and get everyone involved, kids included

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: With the final getting ever closer, the handcuffed couples face a new challenge: their first holiday away together. Jonathan Ross sends them across the UK on iconic British breaks, from beautiful canal trips to camping in the wild countryside, and enjoying all the fun of the fair at the great British seaside. On neutral territory, and without friends and family around, how do they cope with just their cuff-partner for company?

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Sisters Caitlin and Lucy have never been in a romantic relationship with a guy. They decide to visit the restaurant to each take a dive into dating. Naked cleaner Elleigha is confident in everything - except romance. Shes matched with head chef Louis, a man searching for a wild connection with someone with strong opinions. Aspiring erotic-fiction-writer Immogen also attends, finally ready to live out her passions in real life

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Sarah from Plymouth becomes the first returning picker having previously been on the show as David. Now proudly pansexual and living as a woman, Sarah cant wait to road test her new body and hopes her first date as a woman will be everything she has dreamed of. Twenty-year-old plumber Jamie from Swansea is fed up with being hit on by OAPs at work, he comes into the studio looking for love

programme.title: The Eclipse
programme.desc: Mystery drama set in the Aubrac region of France, focusing on events taking place in the picturesque village of Bozouls. The community is buzzing with activity. Manue and Johanna, both gendarmes, get together for the big party to celebrate the eclipse. However tragedy strikes when Nour, the daughter of new rural settlers, disappears after a terrible accident. Manue and Johanna investigate the disappearance, which involves their children. With Anne Charrier, Claire Keim and Hubert Delattre. In French

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.sub-title: Lido, Anna Vincenzos and Le Bistro
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three of the restaurants he tried to save, returning to Lido, Anna Vincenzos and Le Bistro to find out whether his advice has been put to good use

programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: Brenda Blethyn and Jessica Reynolds star in this drama based on the bestseller by Barbara Taylor Bradford, charting a womans rise from maid to businesswoman. In early 1900s Yorkshire, ambitious maid Emma Harte dreams of escaping Fairley Hall for a better life, but is caught up in a forbidden romance with Edwin Fairley, son of the master of the house. In 1970s New York, the worlds richest woman is warned that a plot is brewing to take away the empire she has worked so hard to build. With Ewan Horrocks, Will Mellor, Leanne Best and Lydia Leonard

programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: As housemaid Emma and young master Edwin fall deeper in love, she struggles to see how they can overcome their class differences. Meanwhile, Adam and Olivia continue their own love affair as Adeles erratic behaviour and paranoia worsen. In the 1970s, 80-year-old Emma reckons with her childrens plan to oust her from the Harte empire - unaware of a secret romance blossoming between her protegee granddaughter Paula and Jim Fairley. Starring Jessica Reynolds and Brenda Blethyn

programme.title: Stacey Solomons Renovation Rescue
programme.desc: Stacey Solomon rescues homeowners whove been left high and dry by their builders, costing them their hard-earned cash and leaving them in chaos. Stacey shares her infectious enthusiasm for DIY and her clever money-saving design tips, helping families salvage their half-built home without breaking the bank. Stacey also upscales her own building skills, mastering everything from plumbing to micro-cementing, and puts all this building knowledge into practice at her own family home, Pickle Cottage. Erick and Carolines 1980s bungalow extension in Enfield has left them seriously out of pocket and downhearted. They had a vision of a Japandi - a Japanese/Scandinavian-inspired open-plan kitchen, dining and living area and a colourful bedroom. But its in jeopardy after they lost tens of thousands of pounds on building work that had to be re-done, leaving them facing a massive overspend. Staceys determined to help them realise their dream, by showing them how to tackle jobs themselves to help them save money. Stacey also makes a start on her own DIY project, building a wall in her garage to separate her home gym from her husband Joes fishing gear. Then she helps Erick and Caroline get to grips with plumbing, floor-fitting and laying decking. And she also brings them beautiful, achievable DIY design ideas including a stunning oak-panelled room divider and a striking colour-blocking paint scheme for their bedroom

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: A new cellmate spells danger for Lucas, forcing Dillon to take desperate measures. Theresa pushes Mercedes to give Warren another chance. Diane and Tony continue to lie to protect their loved ones. And a residents romantic hopes end in betrayal. Writers: Emily Gascoyne, Gary Sewell; Dirs: Duncan Foster, Eleri B. Jones; Prod Co: Lime PicturesShort: A new cellmate spells danger for Lucas, forcing Dillon to take drastic action. Theresa pushes Mercedes to give Warren another chance. After a flirtatious evening leads to steamy intimacy in the hot tub with residents Josh and Holly, the couple are left questioning everything. Meanwhile, Chanice and Richardo invite 15 of the residents upstairs for an orgy, though being in a room with so many people having sex proves overwhelming for Richardo, so a once-in-a-lifetime experience seems set to pass the couple by

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebes frank attitude causes Monica and Rachel to lose their tempers. Joey reluctantly accepts a job as a waiter at Central Perk, while Ross and Chandler argue over which of them made up a raunchy joke

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel gives advice on being more independent to her pampered younger sister (Reese Witherspoon), who promptly makes her regret it by flirting with Ross. Meanwhile, Joey gets in trouble for giving free food to female customers at Central Perk

programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, including Im a Celebrity... South Africa, Obsession, Married at First Sight Australia, Britains Got Talent, Absolutely Dyer: Danny and Dani Do Italy, Big Beasts, The Yorkshire Vet, and ITV News on the governments phone alarm. First shown in April 2023

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their insightful and passionate takes on TV shows including Im a Celebrity South Africa, Citadel, The 1% Club, Celebrity Hunted, Britains Got Talent, Reunion Hotel, and Canal Boat Diaries. First shown in May 2023

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Essex boy Bradley, who hasnt been on a date in more than a decade, is paired with assertive Lucy, an architect from Manchester. Next through the door is Ann-Marie, celebrating her 50th birthday. Having survived four bouts of cancer, she believes time is precious and that love is the most important thing. Her evening gets off to a good start when cleaning company owner Carl arrives with a surprise present. Self-confessed serial dater Mitz considers himself a pro, but his unusual technique - involving telling lies - threatens to derail his meeting with Sarah

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Dating show hosted by Anna Richardson in which two singletons choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who are revealed to them one body part at a time. They then they whittle the candidates down until only one remains. In the first edition, Aina, a music producer from London, wants to find someone adventurous who she can have a deeper connection with, while Guildford-based designer Mal - who has dated men and women - is curious to see who she will be drawn to when all options are laid bare before her. The first episode features Aina, a music producer from London, and Mal, a designer from Guildford

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Cam finally gets a chance to shine when he is asked to preside over the high-school graduation ceremony at the last minute, while Jay gives the commencement address

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: As the birth of Haley and Dylans twins approaches, the programme reflects on how all the family members celebrated their birthdays in the past year

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: A new cellmate spells danger for Lucas, forcing Dillon to take desperate measures. Theresa pushes Mercedes to give Warren another chance, Diane and Tony continue to lie to protect their loved ones and a residents romantic hopes end in betrayal

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Theresa pushes Mercedes to give Warren another chance, and a phone call makes Dillon concerned about Lucass wellbeing

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: A new cellmate spells danger for Lucas, forcing Dillon to take desperate measures. Diane and Tony continue to lie to protect their loved ones, and a residents romantic hopes end in betrayal

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: Comedy clips showcasing amusing internet videos featuring sports of the lawnmower, motorcycle and bicycle variety

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Eagle Rock in California to try to change the fortunes of Capri, a grubby restaurant that often remains closed during lunchtime because of the twin owners lazy attitude. A crisis ensues as soon as Gordon instructs the duo to clean the place, revealing that only tough measures are likely to save the business

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.sub-title: There Can Be Only One Highlander Club
programme.desc: Despite Beverly wanting the two boys to bond, Adam and Johnny Atkins compete for the head of the Highlander Club at school; Barry tries to ensure that the JTP doesnt fall out of touch after high school

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Erica, Barry and Adam are not happy when their distant relative Gleb visits from Russia - until they realise he can make their lives easier. Beverly starts working on her cookbook, which causes tension with the Frentas

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitchell learns a valuable life lesson when Murray, a former resident of the house, shows up unexpectedly. Meanwhile, the Dunphys old neighbour-turned-tech-titan Kenneth comes to visit with a big surprise for Phil, and Mannys dad Javier shows up with a big opportunity for his son

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: In one last family trip, everyone heads to Paris so Jay can accept a lifetime achievement award for his work in the closet industry, but his old enemy Earl Chambers finds a way to haunt him. Meanwhile, Claire has a secret rendezvous and Cams lifelong dream of performing as Fizbo on the streets of the French capital is about to become a reality

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: Chris is forced to admit that Brook is ticking all of his boxes, Gia weighs up taking an intimate step with Scott after years of celibacy, while Alissa and David make the most of their mountain escape

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A parliamentary assistant fears talking politics scares men away, but may have met her match in an outspoken journalist. A 69-year-old former roadie is looking for someone to settle down with after surviving cancer, while a Lithuanian spa therapist searches for an older man who she can start a family with

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In this revisit episode, chef Gordon Ramsay goes back to Mojito, the Junction (formerly known as Flamangos) and Bazzini to check in with the owners he helped in the past and find out whether his advice has been put to good use. Will he be pleased with their progress?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart discovers a hidden musical talent with the help of a sympathetic child psychologist, and quickly becomes an acclaimed jazz drummer - causing Lisa to feel envious of his success. With the voices of the White Stripes

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The family goes to Gloria and Jays house for a barbecue and to watch the football, but an incident at school involving Manny and Luke creates tension between Claire and her host. American comedy, starring Ed ONeill and Julie Bowen

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The first day of school arrives and Phil misreads how Claire is coping with an empty house, while Jay and Gloria disagree over what Manny should wear. programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef arrives at the Palace of Versailles, a vast royal hunting lodge which boasts amazing architecture and a great market. He talks about food fashions and seasonality with the director of the Kings allotment, meets a chocolatier who makes enormous chocolate sculptures, which he then casts in bronze or aluminium, and visits Alain Ducasses newly opened Restaurant Ore. Dishes include sole Veronique, roasted spelt and wild mushrooms, and veal chops

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef ends his excursion at the Bois de Boulogne, a park on the outskirts of Paris, coinciding with the hosting of a prestigious steeplechase event at its Auteuil Hippodrome. James meets fellow Yorkshireman and jockey James Revelly and rustles up leeks vinaigrette with salmon and lamb cutlets with a colourful ratatouille. Later, the chef pays a visit to the Michelin-starred Pre Catelan restaurant, where he enjoys his favourite meal of the trip
